git_available.description: Whether git was available at pre-flight (git binary present AND inside a work tree). When false, all git operations are skipped: clean-tree check, per-wave commits, and the PR step. Optional for back-compat with manifests written before 1.3.0.

backend.description: Wave-execution backend selected at pre-flight: 'teams' when CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and team tooling is present, else 'subagent'. Optional for back-compat with pre-1.0.0 manifests.

wave_token_total.description: Sum of every non-null per-agent and verifier total in this wave; null when nothing was reported.

token_usage.description: Best-effort tally of tokens consumed by every dispatched subagent (analyzer, dev agents, wave verifiers, aggregator). Captured from the usage figures the harness surfaces when each subagent completes; entries are null where no figure was surfaced (e.g. teammates on the teams backend). null until the run finalizes the tally. Optional for back-compat with manifests written before 1.5.0.

total_tokens.description: Sum of every non-null per-agent total.

agents_reported.description: Count of dispatched subagents that surfaced a usage figure.

agents_total.description: Count of dispatched subagents in the run.

complete.description: true only when agents_reported == agents_total (every subagent's usage was captured).

code_atlas_sync.description: Outcome of the pre-PR code-atlas index sync (Step 7-bis). null until the step runs. ran=true when /code-atlas:update was invoked; ran=false with a reason when skipped (git not available, or code-atlas not detected). Optional for back-compat with manifests written before 1.4.0.

single_file.description: Single-file invocation with a {file} placeholder, e.g. 'pytest {file}'.

baseline_failing.description: Pre-existing failing tests at pre-flight; subtracted when attributing new failures.

tokenCount.description: Best-effort token usage for one subagent, captured from the harness completion summary. null when no figure was surfaced; individual fields may be null when only a single total is reported.
